questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: In the following sentences, four words or phrases have been underlined. One of them is incorrect. Choose the incorrect word or phrase from the options.</span></p>

<p>Mathematics <u>will taught</u> <u>in</u> <u>his school</u> <u>by Rahul Sir.</u></p>

<p>Read each sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical error in it. The error, if any will be in one part of the sentence. The letter of that part is the answer. If there is no error, the answer is (D). (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).</p>

<p>A lot of travel delay is caused (A) / due to the inefficiency and lack of good management (B) / on behalf of the railways. (C) / No error. (D)</p>

<p><strong>The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options.</strong></p>

<p><span style="">The&nbsp;</span>Taj Mahal / is one of / the most beautiful / creation in the world.</p>

The following sentence has been divided into five segments. The first segment has no error. Select the option that has the segment with the grammatical error.<br />
Due to the pandemic, / the prices of / medicines increased / every day since / last December.

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction:&nbsp;Find out which part has an error and mark it as your answer. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<p style="">I don&#39;t (A) have any (B) money neither.&nbsp;(C) / No error (D)</p>

<p>The sentence below has been divided into three parts. Select the part of the sentence that has an error. If the sentence has no error, select the option &#39;No Error&#39;.</p>

<p>Chasing the white stag through the/ forests, never catching it, of/ course, for it is a creature of legend.</p>
options: Chasing the white stag through the /
course, for it is a creature of legend. /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is '1' i.e. No Error.

Key Points

  • The sentence is grammatically correct and does not contain any errors.
  • The sentence uses proper punctuation and maintains clarity.
  • The phrase "Chasing the white stag through the forests, never catching it, of course, for it is a creature of legend" is coherent and follows standard English conventions.

Correct sentence: Chasing the white stag through the forests, never catching it, of course, for it is a creature of legend.

Additional Information

  • When examining sentences for errors, it is important to check grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure.
  • If a sentence has no errors, the correct option is "No Error."
  • Understanding sentence construction and the intended meaning helps to identify errors effectively.

questions.statement:
<p>&nbsp;Identify the erroneous part in the given sentence and select the most appropriate option to ensure a meaningful sentence.</p>

<p>The feud escalated to the next generation as the fighting for the family estate continued.</p>
options: The feud escalates to the next generation as the fighting about the family estate continued.&nbsp; /
&nbsp;The feud escalated to the next generation as the fight for the family estate continued. /
The feud escalated to the next generation as the fight over the family estate continued.&nbsp; /
The feud escalated to the next generation as the fighting about the family estate continued.&nbsp; /

questions.statement:
<p>The following sentence has been divided into four parts. Identify the part that contains an error.</p>

<p>(A) Taylor, Richard, and / (B) Francis plays for / (C) the state / (D) basketball team.</p>

<p>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</p>

<p>When I was in high school / I participated in several extracurricular activities / such as the debate team and the school newspaper / which help me developed my communication and writing skills.&nbsp;</p>
options: When I was in high school /
which help me developed my communication and writing skills /
such as the debate team and the school newspaper /
I participated in several extracurricular activities /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Directions: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &lsquo;No error&rsquo;.</strong></p>

<p>Chemicals such as pesticides (1)/ and fungicides harm (2)/ both honey bees and wild native bees. (3)/ No error (4)</p>
options: both honey bees and wild native bees. /

questions.statement:
<p>Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>The differences were/ imperceptible to all but/ the most trained eye.</p>

<p>Directions: Determine the part of the sentence containing an error. The error, if any, will be in only one part of the sentence. If there is no error anywhere in the sentence, mark option 4 - No Error as your choice.</p>

<p>As I&nbsp;have practised hard, (A) / I am confident to clear&nbsp;(B) / the cricket trials. (C) / No Error (D)</p>

<p>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</p>

<p>Anant was telling a story about his most recent exploits when a tree branch break the living room window.</p>
options: when a tree branch break the living room window /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Directions:</span>&nbsp;Each item in this section has a sentence with three underlined parts labelled (a), (b) and (c). Read each sentence to find out whether there is any error in any underlined part. Indicate your response in the Answer Sheet against the corresponding letter i.e., (a) or (b) or (c). If you find no error, your response should be indicated as (d).</p>

<p>(a) <u>When a gas is</u> (b) <u>cooled down it turns into a liquid</u> (c) <u>from a process called condensation.</u> (d) <u>No Error</u></p>

The following sentence has been split into segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. If you don&rsquo;t find any error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.<br />
People admired him, for / he lived by the same principals / that he preached.
options: he lived by the same principals /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Directions</span><span style="">: Each item in this section has a sentence with three underlined parts labelled (a), (b) and (c). Read each sentence to find out whether there is any error in any underlined part and indicate your response. on the Answer Sheet against the corresponding letter i.e., (a) or (b) or (c). If you find no error, your response should be indicated as (d).&nbsp;</span></p>

<p>The chemicals(A)/ are&nbsp;<span style="">into</span> converted(B)/&nbsp;new compounds through chemical reactions.(C)/ No error(D)</p>

<p>Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</p>

<p>The cholera is a frightful disease.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">In the given sentence, identify the segment which contains a grammatical error.</span></p>

<p>There aren&#39;t&nbsp;much weekends between now and Christmas.</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &lsquo;No error&rsquo;.</strong></p>

<p>People that are lonely have higher levels of the hormone cortisol.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</span></p>

<div>The bustling market was filled with a vibrant mob&nbsp;of people, each person engaged in their own unique pursuits and interests.</div>
options: with a vibrant mob of people /

questions.statement: <span style="font-weight: 700;">Directions: Out of the following options, identify the sentence with a grammatical error.</span>
options: The girls have tied some luggages with a rope. /
The girls have tied two pieces of luggage with a rope. /
The girls have tied a lot of luggage with a rope. /
The girls have tied the expensive luggage with a rope. /

questions.statement:
<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>I know how hardest you have tried to fit in my shoes.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">In the given sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error.</span></p>

<div>Monika was reprimanded by her sister&nbsp;for coming lately at&nbsp;home.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div>

<p><strong>Select the part of the sentence that has an error. If there is no error, select No error as the answer.</strong></p>

<p>The lotus is one of the most beautiful flowers that grows in this garden.&nbsp;</p>
options: one of the most beautiful flowers&nbsp; /
